UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information in the SkySnap application. This entity serves as the root for user-specific data and relationships to their devices and captures.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity (Firebase User UID).

email.description: The user's email address, used for authentication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

Device.title: Device
Device.description: Represents an ESP32 camera device registered by a user in the SkySnap application. Each device captures image bursts and has configurable settings.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Device entity (ESP32 device ID).

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this device. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Device)

name.description: A user-friendly name assigned to the device (e.g., 'Living Room Cam').

serialNumber.description: The unique hardware serial number of the ESP32 device.

model.description: The model identifier of the ESP32 device (e.g., 'ESP32-S3 Cam').

lastSeen.description: Timestamp of the last successful communication or data transfer from the device.

DeviceSetting.title: DeviceSetting
DeviceSetting.description: Stores the configuration settings for a specific ESP32 camera device, including its WiFi network and processing preferences.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DeviceSetting entity.

deviceId.description: Reference to the Device these settings belong to. (Relationship: Device 1:1 DeviceSetting)

ssid.description: The WiFi network SSID (Service Set Identifier) the device is configured to connect to.

processingMode.description: The preferred processing mode for captures: 'cloud' for Firebase Cloud Functions or 'local' for phone-side processing.

autoWallpaperEnabled.description: A boolean flag indicating if automatic wallpaper updates are enabled for this device's processed images.

lutPreference.description: The identifier or name of the user's preferred Look Up Table (LUT) for color grading processed images.

Capture.title: Capture
Capture.description: Represents a single multi-frame image burst captured by an ESP32 device, including metadata about the capture and its processing status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Capture entity (capture ID).

deviceId.description: Reference to the Device that performed this image capture. (Relationship: Device 1:N Capture)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this capture. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Capture)

status.description: The current processing status of the capture ('pending', 'processing', 'done', 'error').

timestamp.description: The date and time when the image capture was initiated.

frameCount.description: The total number of raw frames captured in this burst.

exposures.description: An array of exposure values (e.g., in milliseconds or EV stops) for each raw frame captured.

rawFramesStoragePath.description: The base path in Firebase Storage where the raw burst frames are stored (e.g., /raw/{uid}/{captureId}/).

processedImageUrl.description: The URL or path to the final processed, high-quality image in Firebase Storage (e.g., /processed/{uid}/{captureId}/final.jpg).

errorMessage.description: Optional field to store details if the processing pipeline encountered an error.

definition.description: Stores user profile information, accessible only by the owning user. The document ID is the Firebase Authentication UID (`userId`).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the user.

definition.description: Stores details for a user's registered ESP32 camera device. The document ID is the unique device identifier. This document includes a denormalized 'userId' field for robust authorization without requiring parent `get()` calls in security rules, fulfilling Authorization Independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the device owner.

params.name: deviceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the ESP32 device.

definition.description: Stores the configuration settings for a specific device. The document ID 'config' is a fixed string, as there is a 1:1 relationship between a device and its settings. This document includes denormalized 'userId' (from the parent user) and 'deviceId' (from the parent device) for authorization independence and clear ownership verification.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the device owner.

params.name: deviceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the ESP32 device these settings apply to.

definition.description: Stores metadata for a multi-frame image burst captured by a device. The document ID is the unique identifier for the capture. This document includes denormalized 'userId' (from the user profile) and 'deviceId' (from the parent device) for authorization independence and direct ownership checks.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication User ID (UID) of the capture owner.

params.name: deviceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the ESP32 device that performed the capture.

params.name: captureId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific image capture burst.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL - Mandate A):** This design eliminates the need for expensive and complex `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules, which is crucial for atomicity and performance. For all user-owned data (`Device`, `DeviceSetting`, `Capture`), the `userId` is explicitly denormalized into each document. For example, a `Capture` document, stored at `/users/{userId}/devices/{deviceId}/captures/{captureId}`, will contain its `userId` field. This allows security rules to evaluate ownership directly by comparing `request.auth.uid` with `resource.data.userId` without performing a lookup on the parent `Device` or `UserProfile` document.

**Query Access Patterns (QAPs - Mandate B & C):** The structure facilitates secure `list` operations without rules acting as filters. By organizing user-specific data under `/users/{userId}/...`, any query starting with a specific `userId` in the path (e.g., `/users/{request.auth.uid}/devices`) implicitly restricts results to the authenticated user's data. Rules can then simply check `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;`, ensuring that users can only list their own devices, settings, or captures, and that Firestore's indexes are utilized efficiently for filtering. This path-based ownership, combined with denormalized `userId` within the documents, makes authorization rules simple, explicit, and performant.

**Structural Segregation (Mandate B):** All documents within a given collection share a homogeneous security posture. For instance, all `Device` documents under `/users/{userId}/devices` are private to that user. There are no mixed-access patterns within a single collection, which simplifies security rules significantly.

**Access Modeling (Mandate C):** The design consistently uses path-based ownership for private, user-owned data. For 1:N relationships (e.g., a `UserProfile` having many `Devices`, or a `Device` having many `Captures`), a clear hierarchical path is maintained (`/users/{userId}/devices/{deviceId}/captures/{captureId}`). For the 1:1 relationship between `Device` and `DeviceSetting`, a fixed document ID (`config`) is used under the device path (`/users/{userId}/devices/{deviceId}/settings/config`) to maintain a clear, single source of truth for device settings.

**Data Clarity and Predictability (Mandate D):** The `Capture` entity utilizes a single `status` field (`pending`, `processing`, `done`, `error`) to explicitly model its state, avoiding ambiguous boolean flags. Wildcards in paths are descriptive (e.g., `{userId}`, `{deviceId}`, `{captureId}`) for clarity and maintainability.
